concept: Imaginative / Having Creative Vision / Seeing Possibilities
description: Having or showing creativity and inventiveness in thought; able to envision what does not yet exist. 'Wiero' names the quality of inner vision—the mind that sees beyond what is to what could be.
sound_symbolism: The word opens inner vision. 'Wi' begins with wonder, attention turning inward. The vowel hiatus 'i.e' opens to inner space, imagination's realm. Then 'ro' forms the vision, possibility taking shape.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wiero kana' (imaginative child). Related to 'phisoe' (creative) but emphasizes vision and possibility rather than actual making.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Imaginative vision saw beauty where others saw ordinary—artists who reimagined everyday objects as works of art.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Imagination serves liberation—the ability to envision different ways of being opens paths to change.
